sanskrit: शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया । आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥
hindiTranslation: धैर्ययुक्त बुद्धिके द्वारा संसारसे धीरेधीरे उपराम हो जाय और परमात्मस्वरूपमें मनबुद्धि को सम्यक् प्रकारसे स्थापन करके फिर कुछ भी चिन्तन न करे ॥
englishTranslation: Little by little, the mind should be quieted by steady discernment. Fix the mind in the true self and think of nothing else.
essence: Stillness comes by degrees when the mind stops feeding itself.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The mind should be calmed gradually with firm understanding. Once it rests in the true self, it should stop chasing thoughts.
simpleInsight: Progress can be gentle. You do not have to win against the mind in one struggle.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna needs more than advice about battle — he needs a mind that can hold still. Krishna now gives the method: steady the mind gradually, place it in the true self, and stop feeding it new thoughts.
modernRelevance: You sit in a parked car after a hard conversation, replaying every sentence. The answer is not to wrestle the mind harder, but to bring it back, gently, until the loop loosens.
translationLiteral: शनैः शनैः (little by little) / उपरमेत् (should become quiet) / बुद्ध्या (by the discerning mind) / धृतिगृहीतया (held by firmness) / आत्मसंस्थम् (established in the true self) / मनः (the mind) / कृत्वा (having made) / न (not) / किञ्चित् अपि (anything at all) / चिन्तयेत् (should think)
insight: It teaches you to calm thought through gradual restraint, not sudden suppression.

quoteVariants.text: Quiet the mind slowly. Hold it steady in the true self.
The mind settles by degrees, not force.
Quiet the mind, little by little.
